v5:
SUMMARY:
This PR updates test inputs to improve branch coverage for LogicConditionNeedOptimizationCheck and claims to update syntax to Java 21, but the newly added test method still uses preview pattern syntax and one switch expression lacks a default case.

POINTS:
- [sevntu-checks/src/test/resources-noncompilable/com/github/sevntu/checkstyle/checks/coding/InputLogicConditionNeedOptimizationCheck2.java:118] The newly added `typeGuardAfterParenthesizedPatternWithMethodCall` method uses `case (String s) && s.isEmpty()` which is **preview syntax** (parentheses around type pattern), not the final Java 21 syntax. The PR description states the goal is to "align with Java 21 syntax," yet this new test case retains the old preview format. Correct final syntax (JEP 441) should be `case String s && s.isEmpty()` without parentheses around the type pattern.
- [sevntu-checks/src/test/resources-noncompilable/com/github/sevntu/checkstyle/checks/coding/InputLogicConditionNeedOptimizationCheck2.java:82] The `typeGuardAfterParenthesizedTrueSwitchExpression2` switch expression is missing a `default` case (ends with `case Object x -> "any";` only). The sibling method `typeGuardAfterParenthesizedTrueSwitchExpression1` was correctly updated to include `default -> "any";`. Without a default, this switch expression is non-exhaustive, which is inconsistent with the other updated switch expressions.
- [sevntu-checks/src/test/resources-noncompilable/com/github/sevntu/checkstyle/checks/coding/InputLogicConditionNeedOptimizationCheck2.java:112] Minor: The new `testCoverageCase` method exercises a useful coverage path (method call in first operand: `getValue() && (a)`), which aligns with the check's branch for `branchContains(operands, 1, TokenTypes.METHOD_CALL)`. This is good coverage improvement.
v6:
SUMMARY:
This PR updates Java 21 pattern matching test inputs and adds coverage for LogicConditionNeedOptimizationCheck. While the core changes are valid, two inconsistencies were introduced in the test code that should be addressed.

POINTS:
- [sevntu-checks/src/test/java/com/github/sevntu/checkstyle/checks/coding/LogicConditionNeedOptimizationCheckTest.java:76-85] The `testPatternDefinition()` method uses hardcoded inline violation strings instead of `getCheckMessage(MSG_KEY, "&&", line, column)`. This breaks consistency with the `test()` method in the same class (which uses `getCheckMessage`) and all other test classes in `checks/coding/`. Using `getCheckMessage` makes tests resilient to message template changes. Please align with project convention.

- [sevntu-checks/src/test/resources-noncompilable/com/github/sevntu/checkstyle/checks/coding/InputLogicConditionNeedOptimizationCheck2.java:81] In `typeGuardAfterParenthesizedTrueSwitchExpression2`, the catch-all uses `case Object x -> "any";` without `default`. Meanwhile, `typeGuardAfterParenthesizedTrueSwitchExpression1` (line 38) uses `default -> "any";`. Since these are switch expressions, `default` is safer (handles null). Please make both methods consistent by using `default -> "any";` in both.

- [sevntu-checks/src/test/resources-noncompilable/com/github/sevntu/checkstyle/checks/coding/InputLogicConditionNeedOptimizationCheck2.java:4-23] The original parenthesized pattern guards (e.g., `case (Integer i) && i == 0:`) that triggered violations at lines 32 and 66 were removed entirely and replaced with nested if-statements. Only one pattern guard remains (`typeGuardAfterParenthesizedPatternWithMethodCall` at line 118). While aligning with Java 21 syntax is valid, consider keeping some parenthesized pattern guard tests in the noncompilable resources since this file specifically validates pattern-matching behavior for this check.
